core_narrative.problem_statement: Golfers are hemorrhaging distance and accuracy because traditional speed training creates a devastating trade-off: you gain swing speed but your slice gets faster, your hook gets worse, and your gains never transfer to the course. You're literally practicing leaving the face open at higher speeds - ingraining bad habits that make your scores go UP, not down.
core_narrative.mechanism_introduction: The Y-Trac Weight System is the first speed trainer with non-axial center of gravity that mimics the feel of a real driver. Unlike speed sticks with axial weight distribution that teach your brain a completely different movement pattern, SpeedTrac's 15 configurations (3 positions x 5 weights) train both speed AND face control simultaneously - so every rep builds muscle memory that actually transfers when you grab your driver.
core_narrative.transformation_promise: Stop choosing between speed and accuracy. With SpeedTrac, you'll gain measurable swing speed while simultaneously training the face control that turns that speed into fairways hit and scores dropped. Train once - gain twice.
core_narrative.proof_framework: The physics are undeniable: when weight sits on the shaft axis (like traditional speed sticks), your brain learns a movement pattern that doesn't transfer to a real driver. SpeedTrac's off-axis weight placement creates the same clubhead dynamics as your driver, so gains made in training show up on the course - not just on a radar gun.

level_1_problem_agitation: I gained 15 mph with speed training but my slice got worse. Now I'm hitting it farther into the trees.
Golfers gain 20 yards but miss 6 fairways per round. They're getting longer AND worse.
You're literally practicing leaving the face open at higher speeds. You're ingraining your bad habit.
Every speed training session without face control is literally practice making your bad habits permanent at higher speeds.
I can swing the speed stick at 120 mph but my driver is still stuck at 100. The gains just don't transfer.
I went from a 10 handicap to a 14 after doing speed training. More distance off the tee but my accuracy went to hell.
The reason your speed training gains don't show up on the course is simple - you're training with a stick that feels nothing like a golf club.
level_2_solution_introduction: What if you could train speed AND face control in every single swing?
The Y-Trac Weight System creates non-axial CG - the same off-center weight feel as a real driver.
15 configurations let you progress from face control training to pure speed - or work both simultaneously.
One tool. One system. Speed gains that actually transfer to the course.
Stop training your brain with a movement pattern that doesn't exist in golf.
When the weight feels like a driver, your gains show up like a driver - on the course, not just the radar.
level_3_proof_points: Non-axial CG mimics real driver dynamics - your brain learns the actual movement pattern needed on the course.
3 weight positions x 5 weight options = 15 configurations for progressive, personalized training.
Face control feedback built into every rep - not an afterthought, not a separate drill.
Compact single-tool design vs. bulky 3-stick systems that are nearly impossible to travel with.
Train the specific motor pattern your body will use on the course - not a lab-created movement that evaporates under pressure.
level_4_differentiation: Speed sticks: Axial weight = wrong motor pattern = gains that don't transfer.
Stack System: $600+ with radar and subscription required just to start training.
SuperSpeed: Three separate clubs - bulky, complex, pure speed focus without face control.
SpeedTrac: One tool, non-axial CG, speed + face control training combined.
Other trainers: 'May cause initial accuracy trade-offs' - SpeedTrac: Accuracy is the point.
level_5_risk_reversal: 14-day returns aren't enough to know if training works. We give you real time to see results.
No hidden costs. No required radar purchase. No ongoing subscriptions.
If your slice gets worse, you're not doing SpeedTrac right - and we'll make sure you are.
Your gains should show up on the scorecard, not just the radar gun.

Respects that golfers are skeptical - they've been burned by gimmicks before. Speaks their language without talking down to them.
